mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors seek yield in risk assets.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly CPI inflation above 250 triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high-inflation periods exceed 1.5% despite CPI > 250 or Rank IC > 0.05 in these regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust economic demand, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved cash flow expectations.
failure_condition: 60d SPX returns in co-expansionary periods are negative or Rank IC < 0.03 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3pp monthly decline in unemployment (UNRATE) signals tightening labor markets, increasing wage pressures and inflation expectations, which drive negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days via higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in tightening labor regimes exceed 1.2% or t-test p > 0.1 for mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 1.5% indicates strong supply-side capacity utilization, boosting corporate margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through improved earnings visibility.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high-industrial growth periods are negative or Rank IC < 0.02 in 1-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal weakening consumer demand, reducing corporate revenue expectations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in co-contracting demand regimes exceed -0.8% or Rank IC > 0.04 in 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 100k monthly increase in payrolls (PAYEMS) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) indicates labor market imbalance, creating uncertainty that drives negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in labor mismatch regimes exceed -1.0% or t-test p > 0.05 for mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: A 50bp increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) during a 6-month GDP contraction (GDPC1 < 0) signals aggressive monetary tightening in a weak economy, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in tightening-recession regimes exceed -0.5% or Rank IC > 0.03 in 2-year windows.
